intro: Leibnitzia lyrata, also known as Lyre-leaved Gilia, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a promising treatment for various ailments, such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress-related diseases. The bioactive constituents of Leibnitzia lyrata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Leibnitzia lyrata is often prepared as a tincture or decoction, and it is also used as a topical application for its wound-healing and anti-inflammatory properties.
